column8: Diamonds are stones that are usually clear and extremely hard. They are often very__________ . (BEAUTY)
column9: They're also quite rare, and as a result, they're very___________ . (EXPENSE)
column10: Diamonds make____________jewellery. They are found in rings, necklaces, earrings and watches. (LOVE)
column11: The people who cut and polish rough diamonds to turn them into jewellery are called diamond__________ . (CUT)
column12: Diamonds were probably first discovered in India thousands of years ago. Today, thought, the country's mines are almost____________empty. (COMPLETE)
column13: These days, diamonds are found in many countries around the world. Some of the largest_____________of diamonds are Russia, Canada, Australia and South Africa. (PRODUCE)
column14: Being in a courtroom was a new experience for Alan. He felt more_____________than he had ever felt before. (NERVE)
column15: He looked at the judge. She looked very serious and_________ . (POWER)
column16: Then he looked at the 12 members of the jury. They were sitting there____________with serious expressions on their faces, too. (QUIET)
column17: Then he looked at the______________of his family. His mum smiled at him. (DIRECT)
column18: "This is__________", thought Alan's mum. "It's the proudest day of my life". (WONDER)
column19: "This is fantastic", thought Alan. "I think I'm going to enjoy working as a_________ . (LAW)
